answer:
The correct answer is A. Adjusted Gross Income is calculated by subtracting business expenses from gross income, and taxable income is then found by subtracting exemptions and non-business expenses from adjusted gross income. Working backward: Taxable income ($15,000) plus exemptions ($1,500) plus non-business expenses ($3,000) equals adjusted gross income of $19,500. Adding business expenses ($6,000) to adjusted gross income gives gross income of $25,500.

\boxed{A}
